article_title: New 'Jerk' Seismic Method Predicted 92 Percent of Volcano Eruptions Over a Decade, Now Heading to Mount Etna

content_preview.title: New 'Jerk' Seismic Method Predicted 92 Percent of Volcano Eruptions Over a Decade, Now Heading to Mount Etna
content_preview.summary: Researchers from IPGP and GFZ have validated a single-seismometer technique that detects nanometer-scale ground signals hours before eruptions, and plan to deploy it on Mount Etna in 2026 through the POS4dyke project.
content_preview.body_excerpt: A seismic detection technique that uses a single broadband seismometer to spot the faint ground motions preceding volcanic eruptions has been [validated over more than a decade of real-time, automated operation](https://phys.org/news/2026-01-jerk-volcano-early-method-seismometer.html) at Piton de la Fournaise on the French island of La Reunion. The method, dubbed "Jerk," correctly flagged 92 percent of the 24 eruptions that occurred between 2014 and 2023, with warning times ranging from minutes ...
